items.title: RBI announces results of Fourth Edition of its Global Hackathon – Ha RBI nger
AST SpaceMobile shares drop after its satellite is placed in wrong orbit by Bezos' Blue Origin
items.summary: Blue Origin acknowledged the satellite was placed in the wrong orbit, but has not commented since it was deemed lost.

items.title: Testing the waters? Why Airtel is raising the tariff of a high-value prepaid plan
items.summary: The telecom major’s latest price tweak may be a calculated gamble to gauge subscriber ‘stickiness’ before broader, industry-wide hikes, experts told Mint.

items.title: West Asia crisis poses risks to India's trade and macroeconomic stability: NITI Aayog report
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are impacting India's trade and economy. NITI Aayog reports these tensions widen the current account deficit and pressure the exchange rate. The India-Gulf Cooperation Council Free Trade Agreement is also slowed. Despite challenges, India's merchandise and services trade show resilience.

items.title: American Airlines falls after company dismisses talk of United megamerger
items.summary: American Airlines stock fell after the company dismissed talk of a potential merger with United, citing antitrust concerns and potential harm to competition.

items.title: Eli Lilly agrees to acquire cancer drug maker Kelonia in deal worth up to $7 billion
items.summary: Kelonia is developing technology to reprogram patients' T-cells inside the body so those cells can attack cancer, called in vivo CAR-T.

items.title: Core sector output fell 0.4% in March, lowest in 19 months
items.summary: India’s core sector output contracted 0.4% year-on-year in March, marking its lowest level in 19 months, driven by a sharp decline in fertiliser production amid the West Asia conflict and weaker performance across energy-related industries, according to official data released on Monday.

items.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
items.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

items.title: Broker’s call: Angel One (Add)
items.summary: JM Financial

items.title: Kevin Warsh would be the first tech bro Fed chair. How Silicon Valley shaped him
items.summary: The nominee to lead the Fed is an AI optimist who counts tech titans Peter Thiel and Marc Andreessen among his friends.

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks open muted as US-Iran tensions escalate
items.summary: Indian markets opened with caution on Monday. Major Wall Street indexes saw a muted start following last week's record rally. Escalating United States-Iran tensions are impacting investor sentiment. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all experienced slight declines at the opening bell. Investors are closely watching geopolitical developments.

items.title: RBI draft for upper layer non-banks affects CICs disproportionately, raises compliances costs
items.summary: New Reserve Bank draft on upper layer non-bank finance companies disproportionately impacts core investment companies by increasing compliance costs. Mandatory listing requirements could prove onerous for many CICs, particularly those not seeking public market access. The AUM-based approach may present challenges for these entities, especially regarding listing and governance.

items.title: Motilal Oswal initiates coverage on T&D stocks; 'Buy' on CG Power, GE Vernova T&D, Siemens Energy on capex boom
items.summary: Motilal Oswal identifies opportunities in T&D stocks driven by a rising demand for transformers and HVDC projects. The firm expects companies to benefit from increased capex and revenue growth, maintaining 'Buy' ratings for key players with ambitious price targets.

items.title: Another flight leaves passengers behind due to border delays
items.summary: New European border rules have caused delays at airports across the continent, affecting flights.

items.title: Investors are misreading news about the Iran war, analysts say as markets whipsaw
items.summary: Monday's declines put the brakes on another rapid recovery in global stock indices akin to last year's 'liberation day' bounce.

items.title: MCX to launch coal exchange
items.summary: The exchange is aimed at developing a regulated, transparent, technology-driven market platform for buying and selling coal

items.title: Govt reviews rural schemes, supply chain woes to protect beneficiaries from Iran war impact
items.summary: The Rural Development Ministry is reviewing its key schemes in response to the West Asia conflict's potential impact on global supply chains and prices. The ministry is ensuring uninterrupted employment and timely fund flow for beneficiaries. Measures are being taken to protect rural housing and road projects. This includes timely fund releases and material bank creation.

items.title: Broker’s call: Sagility (Buy)
items.summary: Elara Securities

items.title: Vedanta fixes May 1 as record date for business split
Certus Capital to invest ₹2000 cr in housing, mixed-use projects in FY27
items.summary: The push comes amid a surge in capital flows into Indian real estate, with investments hitting a record $14.3 billion in 2025, up 25% year-on-year

items.title: Radisson Hotel Group expects to create 65,000-80,000 job opportunities in India by 2030 with 500 properties
items.summary: Radisson Hotel Group is embarking on an ambitious journey to enhance its footprint in India, targeting the launch of 500 hotels by 2030. This initiative is projected to create up to 80,000 jobs, focusing on upscale and mid-scale offerings in diverse city environments. Committed to local community enrichment, the group is also prioritizing skill development to foster career growth.

items.title: Cummins India among 7 stocks that hit 52-week high; rally up to 32% in a month
items.summary: Indian stock markets saw a positive start to the week, with the Sensex inching up. A notable trend emerged as seven companies within the BSE 100 index reached their 52-week highs, signaling strong investor confidence and potential for further growth. Several prominent energy and infrastructure stocks, including Adani Power and Tata Power, were among those hitting new peaks.

items.title: IIT Kharagpur launches Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence AI centre to transform mining operations across India
items.summary: IIT Kharagpur has launched the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence for AI-Enabled Geological & Mining Systems, funded by a Rs 15 crore commitment. This new center aims to revolutionize India's mining sector by integrating AI across operations, from exploration to ESG analytics, transforming data-rich but information-poor environments into efficient, data-driven systems.

items.title: Vedanta announces demerger record date, shareholders to get 1:1 shares in new entities
items.summary: Vedanta's board has approved May 1, 2026, as the effective and record date for its demerger. Shareholders will receive shares in newly carved-out businesses, including aluminium, power, and oil and gas, in proportion to their existing holdings. This significant restructuring will transform Vedanta into separate listed entities.

items.title: IEX shares tumble 8% after CERC draft on market coupling raises concerns
items.summary: The decline followed the release of a draft proposal by the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission on electricity price discovery, which introduces market coupling norms that could significantly alter the dynamics of power exchanges in India

items.title: 'Resumption of hostilities': Seized ship, vessel attacks push U.S.-Iran ceasefire toward brink
items.summary: A U.S. seizure of an Iranian cargo ship and reports of vessels coming under fire in the Gulf have pushed a fragile ceasefire with Tehran to the brink.

items.title: RBI allows NDF contracts to clients, rebooking of related party trades
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has made a pivotal move by retracting its earlier April 1 directive, which had restricted banks from offering non-deliverable forward contracts and barred the rebooking of cancelled foreign exchange derivative contracts. This reversal allows banks to enjoy increased operational flexibility, fostering a more fluid trading environment.

items.title: Quote of the day by Warren Buffett: “You should never test the depth of the water with both feet.”
items.summary: In turbulent markets, Warren Buffett's advice to "never test the depth of the water with both feet" emphasizes cautious, gradual investment. This strategy involves phased entries, diversification, and thorough analysis to mitigate risks, prioritizing capital preservation and informed decision-making over impulsive actions amidst global uncertainties.

items.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks open muted amid US-Iran war tensions
Renault Duster secures 5-star BNCAP safety rating: Full scores, crash test results, features explained
items.summary: The Renault Duster has earned a 5-star safety rating from BNCAP. It scored highly in adult and child occupant protection tests. This rating applies to multiple variants. The SUV features advanced safety systems like ESC and ADAS. These include Adaptive Cruise Control and Autonomous Emergency Braking. The Duster offers strong structural integrity and occupant protection.

items.title: Insurance firms boost stakes in 10 mid-cap stocks in Q4FY26
items.summary: Insurance companies increased their stakes in 10 mid-cap stocks in Q4FY26, signalling rising confidence and a strategic shift toward higher equity exposure.

items.title: Surya Global Flexifilms accelerates its vision of a digitally intelligent enterprise with SAP S/4HANA RISE with SAP, partnering with SAVIC
items.summary: Surya Global Flexifilms is undertaking a comprehensive digital transformation by implementing SAP S/4HANA RISE with SAP, guided by CEO Mannas Agarwal's vision for a data-driven, agile manufacturing leader. This strategic initiative, in partnership with SAVIC, aims to integrate core functions, leverage AI-driven insights, and enhance operational efficiency for global competitiveness.

items.title: FY27 capex growth of states pegged at 8–10%: report
items.summary: State capital spending growth is set to slow in FY27. This moderation follows a strong FY26. Rising revenue expenses and slower revenue growth are key factors. Geopolitical issues in West Asia could further impact state finances. Fiscal discipline will be crucial for states balancing welfare and investment needs. Top states continue to prioritize infrastructure development.

items.title: Vedanta demerger: Anil Agarwal-led mining major fixes May 1 as record date, announces share allotment details
items.summary: Vedanta demerger: Anil Agarwal-led mining major, Vedanta Limited, on Monday, 20 April, announced that its board has decided to make its proposed demerger effective from 1 May.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Trent and Yes Bank among 7 big movers on Monday
items.summary: Markets ended flat, but action was stock-specific, with Trent, Adani Power, Deccan Gold Mines and IEX among the top movers, while Lenskart and Yes Bank saw pressure amid mixed sector cues.

items.title: India bonds edge up in choppy session as traders weigh truce prospects
items.summary: Indian government bonds inched higher ​in a choppy session ​on Monday, with elevated crude oil prices and uncertainty over ​a truce expiring this week keeping traders cautious.

items.title: BPTP to invest Rs 1,100 cr to build luxury homes in Haryana's Faridabad
items.summary: Realty firm BPTP Ltd will invest around Rs 1,100 crore to develop a luxury housing project in Faridabad as part of its expansion plan.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slide as US-Iran tensions flare; Brent jumps 8%
items.summary: US stock futures are down 0.4% to 0.5% as tensions in the Middle East escalate, casting uncertainty over peace talks. The US Navy's seizure of an Iranian vessel has renewed fears over oil transport, coinciding with an impending ceasefire deadline.

items.title: Hyundai Venue Knight Edition launched in India: Price, features, variants and engine details
items.summary: Hyundai has launched the Venue Knight Edition in India. This special version features a bold, dark-themed design. It includes an all-black exterior with unique badging. The interior also sports a dark theme with brass accents. A dashcam is now available on select variants. The engines remain the same as the standard model. Prices range from Rs 9.

items.title: USA Rare Earth to buy Brazil's Serra Verde for $2.8 billion to build supply outside Asia
items.summary: USA Rare Earth on Monday announced plans to buy Brazilian rare earths miner Serra Verde in a deal worth $2.8 billion in cash and shares.

items.title: Gold falls on inflation fears, firmer dollar amid renewed US-Iran tensions
items.summary: Gold hits lowest level in a week

items.title: Exporters’ support lift orthodox leaf prices in Coonoor tea auctions
items.summary: Global Tea Auctioneers said the sales percentage in leaf grades was 84% out of the offered quantity of 14,08,201 kg, while dust grades maintained 89% sales out of the offered quantity of 4,74,137 kg.

items.title: India’s core sector growth contracts 0.4% in March 2026 to lowest level since August 2024
items.summary: March 2026 brought unexpected challenges for India's foundational industries, as the Index of Eight Core Industries reported a contraction of 0.4 percent. The sharp declines in essential sectors such as fertiliser, crude oil, coal, and electricity contributed significantly to this downturn, reversing the positive momentum that had been evident just a month prior.

items.title: GIFT City grants first family office permit
items.summary: Permit granted to Poornam Asset Management IFSC ‌Pvt Ltd

items.title: Oil prices jump after Iran and U.S. attack commercial ships as tensions escalate over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Crude oil prices surged Sunday, as the U.S. and Iran teetered on the brink of a renewed war after attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs to Two-Month High Amid Middle East Deal Optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin rose to the highest level since early February after a flurry of comments from the US and Iran sparked optimism that the conflict in the Middle East may be heading toward a resolution.

items.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
items.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

items.title: European stocks slide as Gulf tanker attacks threaten fragile ceasefire
items.summary: European stocks traded lower on Monday as traders assess a re-acceleration in U.S.-Iran tensions.

items.title: Got extra cash to invest: Should you put it in an NFO? Check pros, cons and right approach to choose
items.summary: An NFO is the first-time launch of a mutual fund scheme by an asset management company (AMC). It is the period during which investors can subscribe to the fund before it becomes available for regular investment.

items.title: Fish waste finds new life as high-value biomedical material
items.summary: Scientists at ICAR-Central Institute of Fisheries Technology (ICAR-CIFT), Kochi, have developed a patented technology that converts fish scale waste into nanofibre-based graft materials capable of accelerating bone and dental healing.

items.title: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry
items.summary: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry

items.title: Stock Market Highlights 20 April 2026: Sensex inches up 26 pts to 78,520, Nifty stays above 24,300 as markets end flat
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Indian markets ended flat on Monday, with Sensex and Nifty gaining marginally as US-Iran tensions, rising oil prices and global uncertainty kept investors cautious and volatility concerns persisted.

items.title: Nifty holds 24,300, but bulls struggle to break past 24,500 resistance
items.summary: Nifty 50 ends at 24,364, up just 11 points or 0.05%, while Sensex inches up 26 points to close at 78,520

items.title: No technical snag on Hyderabad-Hubballi flight, diversion to Bangalore due to bad weather, says FLY91
items.summary: Regional airline FLY91 clarified that its flight IC3401 from Hyderabad to Hubballi did not face any technical problems. Reports suggesting a snag were denied by the airline. The flight was diverted to Bengaluru due to adverse weather conditions upon nearing Hubballi. Safety was never compromised, and the diversion followed standard operating procedures.

items.title: Pernod Ricard is said to begin IPO process for India unit
items.summary: Pernod Ricard SA, the maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Regal whisky, has begun work on a potential initial public offering of its Indian business, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: India growth resilient, markets near correction: HDFC Securities
items.summary: India's economy is set for robust growth. Real GDP is projected at 6.5 percent for FY26-FY27. Nominal GDP will expand between 10 to 11 percent. Inflation is expected to ease to 4.5 percent. Fiscal deficit targets are set at 4.3 percent. Retail investor participation remains strong. Markets may be nearing the bottom of the current correction cycle.

items.title: Rupee falls most in a week as traders brace for US-Iran ceasefire expiry
items.summary: The Indian rupee logged its steepest one-day fall ​in a week on Monday, ​with wary investors bracing for the end of the ​U.S.-Iran ceasefire a day later amid rising tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: Meesho allots over 94.79 lakh equity shares to employees under ESOP
items.summary: Following this allotment, the issued, subscribed, and paid-up equity share capital of the Bengaluru-headquartered company has increased from ₹456,40,55,196 to ₹4,57,35,34,576

items.title: How the Iran war affects your money and bills
items.summary: The conflict in the Middle East has increased pressure on the cost of petrol, household energy bills and even food.

items.title: Euro zone yields nudge higher on US-Iran ceasefire worries
items.summary: Euro zone bond yields ticked higher on Monday, as concerns ​grew that the ceasefire between the United States ​and Iran might not hold, though borrowing costs were still meaningfully below their ​late-March highs hit before the ceasefire was announced.

items.title: JAL insolvency case: Lenders flag ‘leak’ that may have aided Vedanta bid
items.summary: Following the alleged leak, Vedanta submitted a revised bid with improved net present value and equity infusion, which was rejected for missing the deadline.

items.title: Oil prices rise 5% on fears of US-Iran ceasefire collapse
items.summary: Oil prices jumped around 5% in early Monday trading on ​fears that the ceasefire between ​the United States and Iran could collapse after the U.S. ​seized an Iranian cargo ship and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely halted.

items.title: Will rising costs stall Bajaj Consumer’s strong recovery?
items.summary: The personal care major made an impressive comeback in FY26, but global headwinds are starting to test this newfound resilience.

items.title: Bank of Maharashtra Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 34% YoY to Rs 2,014 crore; NII up 20%
items.summary: ​ Bank of Maharashtra reported a 35% YoY rise in Q4FY26 net profit to Rs 2,014 crore, driven by strong growth in NII, advances, and overall business. Asset quality improved, with lower gross and net NPAs. For FY26, profit rose 27%, while deposits and advances grew robustly. The bank also announced a total dividend of 22% for the year.

items.title: Rupee falls 19 paise to settle at 93.10 against US dollar
items.summary: Forex traders say rupee remained under pressure due to a fresh standoff between the US and Iran that led to a closure of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global supply

items.title: Crypto Hack Sparks $9 Billion Outflows From Biggest DeFi Lender
items.summary: A weekend hack that saw almost $300 million drained from a little-known crypto project has triggered a crisis of confidence among decentralized-finance investors, with users pulling billions of dollars from DeFi’s biggest lending platform.

items.title: Reliance, Essar top bidders for coal-based methane gas exploration blocks
items.summary: The bidding frenzy for coal-bed methane blocks heats up as Reliance Industries and the Essar Group step into the ring. The Directorate General of Hydrocarbons has put 16 blocks up for grabs in two competitive rounds, attracting the attention of Oil India while ONGC chooses to withdraw.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: BoM & Groww Q4 profit soar, Navkar Corporation turns profitable, PNB Housing, Nelco, Indosolar, Indbank Merchant to announce Q4, ICICI & HDFC Bank stocks end flat, Yes Bank shares down
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, April 20, 2026, Live Updates: Catch live updates

items.title: Fed nominee Warsh prepares for monetary policy road test before Senate panel
items.summary: After more than a decade criticizing ​the U.S. central bank, former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh faces a mark-to-market moment in ​a Senate hearing on Tuesday when lawmakers will likely press the Fed chief nominee to flesh out his monetary policy and economic ideas and calls for fundamental change.

items.title: Vedanta vs Adani: Jaiprakash case may test limits of ‘commercial wisdom’
items.summary: Lawyers say the Anil Agarwal-led Vedanta's challenge is serious and, if successful, could force lenders to better explain their decisions, especially when choosing a lower-value plan based on factors like upfront cash and faster payments.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 20: Triveni Turbine, Radico Khaitan, Ather Energy, JSW Cement, Trent among top gainers
items.summary: On April 20, the Indian stock market remained stable, with Nifty 50 and Sensex closing flat. Broader indices fell. Geopolitical tensions rose as the US seized an Iranian cargo ship, leading to a potential Iranian retaliation and increased oil prices.

items.title: Buy every dip, markets have turned a corner; 2 trading calls for near-term gains: CA Rudramurthy BV
items.summary: India's stock market shows a fundamental shift, with analysts advising investors to buy on dips as negative news is now ignored. The Nifty is projected to reach 24,800-25,000, driven by strong sector performance and returning FII buying. Traders are also presented with specific near-term opportunities in Varun Beverages and GNFC.

items.title: India is making a big move to outflank China in Sri Lanka
items.summary: India and China appear to be locked in an escalating energy rivalry in Sri Lanka. India plans a new energy hub at Trincomalee with the UAE, including an oil pipeline. This initiative aims to secure fuel supply chains and counter China's expanding presence in Sri Lanka's energy market. The project seeks to reshape regional energy dependence.

items.title: Sensex inches up 27 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,350; mid, small-caps underperform
items.summary: The Sensex closed at 78,520.30, rising modestly by 27 points, or 0.03%, while the Nifty 50 ended at 24,364.85, gaining 11 points, or 0.05%. The Nifty Midcap 100 index slipped 0.18%, while the Nifty Smallcap 100 index dropped 0.45%.

items.title: Nvidia rival tells CNBC it's seeking at least $100 million in funding as European AI chip market booms
items.summary: Investor interest for AI chip startups is rising, but big challenges remain for the nascent sector.

items.title: Adani Power eclipses Adani Ports to becomes Group’s most valuable stock with m-cap Rs 3.99 lakh crore
items.summary: Adani Power Limited surged nearly 5% on Monday to hit fresh 52-week high, becoming the most valuable Adani Group firm. The company, with a ₹3.99 lakh crore m-cap, surpassed Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone. Strong stock momentum, new subsidiary formation, and reaffirmed ratings supported investor sentiment on the Street.

items.title: What's happening to UK petrol and diesel prices?
items.summary: UK petrol and diesel prices have started to fall after 46 consecutive days of rises at the pump.

items.title: UniCredit boss plots Commerzbank shake-up as lender continues its takeover pursuit of German rival
items.summary: UniCredit's CEO said the combination would deliver "significant cross-border value" and investment "firepower".

items.title: Trading Account vs Demat Account: Key differences every investor must know
items.summary: A trading account executes buy and sell orders, while a demat account stores securities electronically. Both accounts are essential for stock market investing, with the demat account providing security and the trading account facilitating transactions.

items.title: MMRDA secures 216 acres in Raigad for Mumbai 3.0 project through participatory model
items.summary: Mumbai 3.0 project is underway with MMRDA securing 216 acres in Raigad district. This marks a significant step in a new participatory development model. Landowners are offered various compensation options. The project aims to create a new economic hub with advanced infrastructure. It is expected to generate substantial employment and attract investment, shaping a new urban future for the region.

items.title: Nippon India MF launches ‘Har Ghar Investor’ campaign
items.summary: The Har Indian Investor campaign has been launched to educate the retail investors and instil confidence while they take their first step in investing

items.title: Fire at Rajasthan refinery site a day before PM Modi’s inauguration
items.summary: A fire flared up at the Pachpadra Refinery-cum-Petrochemical Complex in Balotra, with emergency services reacting promptly to contain the situation. This alarming event takes place on the eve of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's planned trip to Rajasthan, where he will unveil India's inaugural greenfield integrated refinery-petrochemical complex. This initiative is poised to revolutionize the energy sector in India.

items.title: Apple withholds data in India antitrust case, CCI sets final hearing
items.summary: India's antitrust watchdog is moving quickly to decide penalties for Apple. The Competition Commission of India found Apple abused its dominant position in the iPhone app market. Apple has not provided requested financial data. The watchdog has set a final hearing for May 21. This signals a hardening stance against the US tech giant. Apple faces potential fines in India.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares slide as Middle East ceasefire hangs in balance
items.summary: European shares declined on Monday, on concerns that a ​ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran might collapse after Washington said it had seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate.

items.title: JNU: 5% supernumerary quota for teaching, non-teaching staff kids from 2026-27
items.summary: Jawaharlal Nehru University has introduced a five per cent supernumerary quota for children of its teaching and non-teaching staff. This new provision will apply to all undergraduate and postgraduate courses starting from the upcoming academic session. The university will create additional seats to accommodate this quota, ensuring the existing seat matrix remains unaffected.

items.title: India's D2C growth powered by Tier 2, 3 cities with 66 per cent new orders in FY26
items.summary: India's direct-to-consumer brands are seeing rapid expansion driven by smaller cities. Tier 2 and 3 cities will account for nearly 66 percent of new orders in FY26. This growth fuels a 33 percent rise in order volumes and a 32 percent increase in GMV. The D2C market is set to reach USD 60 billion by 2030.

items.title: SAEL begins operation of 600-MW solar power plant in Andhra Pradesh
items.summary: SAEL Industries has started a 600-megawatt solar power plant in Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh. This plant will supply power to the national grid for 25 years. It uses over 12 lakh solar modules. The project will reduce carbon emissions significantly. This development adds to SAEL's growing operational capacity.

items.title: ICICI Bank’s strong Q4 revives re-rating hopes despite cost pressures
items.summary: Strong loan growth and sharply lower provisions boost profit, underpinning a re-rating case even as elevated opex and soft fee income weigh on operating leverage

items.title: Gold price prediction: Yellow metal to hit $5,000 soon? ETF inflows jump even as inflation fears rise
items.summary: Gold prices fell due to a stronger dollar and escalating Middle Eastern tensions, impacting inflation expectations. Central banks continue to support gold demand, while silver faces a structural supply deficit.

items.title: The Shift to conscious living: Inside India’s new luxury real estate paradigm
items.summary: Indian luxury homebuyers are prioritizing wellness and sustainability, shifting from extravagance to eco-smart designs and green spaces. Buyers now seek health-focused, well-ventilated homes with spacious layouts and environmentally responsible amenities. This trend is evident in developments offering holistic lifestyles, combining open green areas with urban accessibility, reflecting a reordering of priorities towards enduring comfort and quality of living.

items.title: Gold-Silver ratio falls below 60. What does it signal about gold and silver prices?
items.summary: A drop in the gold-silver ratio below 60 typically signals that silver is outperforming gold, often seen in the mid-to-late stages of a precious metals bull cycle when speculative, industrial, and safe-haven demand for silver accelerates, analysts said.

items.title: MCX gets SEBI nod to set up coal exchange subsidiary
items.summary: The move builds on MCX's existing energy derivatives business, which includes highly liquid contracts on crude oil and natural gas, and an electricity futures contract launched last year

items.title: Alivaa Hotels bets on unbranded market for growth to 80 properties by FY27; seeks to raise $4 million
items.summary: Alivaa Hotels is capitalizing on India's unbranded hotel market by franchising small properties. With a unique performance-based revenue model, the company aims for 80 operational hotels by the end of the fiscal year.

items.title: Multibagger Diamond Power stock rises 9%, extends winning streak to sixth session; up 39% in April
items.summary: Diamond Power Infrastructure's stock surged over 9.5% to ₹164, marking a seven-month high. The company secured multiple orders, including a ₹45 crore deal with Adani Power, boosting investor sentiment and revenue outlook.

items.title: Bitcoin consolidates near $74,500 after hitting 10-week high of $78,000 as Hormuz tensions weigh on sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin is consolidating near recent highs after a strong rally, as geopolitical tensions around the Strait of Hormuz trigger short-term volatility. Despite the pullback, steady ETF inflows and improving institutional demand indicate a constructive outlook, with analysts expecting a potential breakout if key resistance levels are breached in the near term.

items.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei climbs as AI optimism outweighs Mideast concerns
items.summary: Japan's ​Nikkei share average rose on ​Monday, nearing the all-time high it scaled ​last week, as optimism over the red-hot artificial intelligence sector outweighed concerns about the Middle East crisis.

items.title: Soaring oil prices put gold rate today under pressure. Is it the right time to buy gold?
items.summary: Gold prices have eased amid soaring crude oil prices, but analysts believe the current weakness provides an ideal setup for long-term investing. Axis Direct predicts a potential 15% price rise by 2026, although recent conflicts have led to short-term price drops.

items.title: India’s truckers brace for first diesel price hike in four years
items.summary: In the backdrop of impending elections, truck fleet operators across India are seeing storm clouds on the horizon with expected fuel rationing and a steep rise in diesel prices. The flare-up in the Middle East is sending shockwaves through global oil supplies, forcing state refiners to reconsider their strategies amid rising inflation.

items.title: Indian market faces AI narrative problem; it'll be smallcaps over largecaps for next 5 years: Manish Gunwani
items.summary: Indian stock market valuations are attractive. However, global investor focus on Artificial Intelligence is diverting capital away from emerging markets like India. Foreign institutional investor flows are expected to remain subdued unless the AI theme slows or India implements significant structural reforms. Bandhan AMC is investing in manufacturing exporters and metals. The IT sector faces a long-term slowdown.

items.title: China calls for ‘concerted’ industry efforts to tackle excess solar production
items.summary: China is the undisputed global leader in solar energy, making more than 80% of the world's solar panel components.

items.title: Clarity Labs raises ₹4 crore in seed funding led by Artha Venture Fund II
items.summary: Artha will collaborate closely with Clarity Labs on product and portfolio strategy, brand building and go-to-market execution across direct-to-consumer platforms, marketplaces and quick commerce

items.title: Groww reports 87% revenue jump in Q4, posts ₹686.4 crore profit as Board sees Director exit
items.summary: For the full financial year FY26, consolidated revenue from operations reached ₹4,644.6 crore against ₹3,901.7 crore in FY25, while annual net profit came in at ₹2,083 crore

items.title: Power transmission stocks rally—but are Hitachi, GE still good buys?
items.summary: A strong transmission cycle is driving demand, but after steep gains, future returns will hinge on order wins and margin delivery

items.title: Basmati exporters of Punjab urge Goyal to restructure & initiate reforms in development body BEDF
items.summary: The BEDF has been under limelight ever since it hiked the mandatory contract registration fee by more than 100 per cent last year

items.title: PM Modi to inaugurate India's 1st greenfield integrated refinery-cum-petrochem complex in Rajasthan
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to arrive in Rajasthan this Tuesday, where he will unveil a transformative project—the country’s inaugural greenfield integrated refinery-cum-petrochemical complex at Pachpadra.

items.title: FIIs slash stakes in 8 stocks over two quarters, shares crack up to 50%. Are you holding any?
items.summary: FIIs have steadily reduced their stakes in about 104 companies over the past two quarters, December 2025 (October–December) and March 2026 (January-March).

items.title: Deccan Gold Mines shares rally 16% on India’s first private gold mining project. Check details
items.summary: Shares of Deccan Gold Mines Ltd surged 16% as the Jonnagiri Gold Project, India’s first private gold mining venture, nears commercial production. The development boosts earnings visibility and supports domestic supply, amid strong investor interest in the company’s expanding gold and critical minerals portfolio.

items.title: India, South Korea to upgrade trade pact, deepen tech and supply chain ties
items.summary: India and South Korea will resume talks to upgrade their economic partnership agreement. The goal is to increase bilateral trade to 50 billion dollars by 2030. Both nations will also enhance cooperation in critical technologies and supply chains. An economic security dialogue will be established. Discussions will cover shipbuilding, steel, and sustainability. Industrial collaboration will expand through new agreements.

items.title: Dharavi project begins; 11,000 rehab homes being constructed on railway land in Matunga
Mehul Telecom IPO Day 2: Issue subscribed over 6x so far; NIIs steal the show. Check GMP, issue details
items.summary: Mehul Telecom IPO, open from April 17-21, features shares priced at ₹96-98. The company reported a profit rise and plans to use funds for working capital and expansion. Strong investor interest is evident with a subscription status of 6.60x as of day 2.

items.title: Asia markets mostly rise as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate after ship seizure
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets were mostly higher Monday as a U.S. seizure of an Iranian vessel deepened tensions, keeping investors on edge.

items.title: Gallantt Ispat shares surge 60% so far in April. Is there more steam left?
items.summary: Gallantt Ispat shares have soared 60% in April, outpacing market trends. Even on a longer timeframe, the stock's returns have been impressive. The stock's last three and five-year returns are 1,284% and 1,715%, respectively.

items.title: Penny stock under ₹20 hits upper circuit after announcement of 50% dividend, Q4 results 2026 date
items.summary: Penny stock under ₹20: AVI Polymers share price has fallen 37% in one month, but has rallied 82% in three months. The circuit-to-circuit stock has jumped 125% in six months and has rallied 114% in one year. The smallcap stock has delivered multibagger returns of 195% in two years.

items.title: Stock to buy for short term: Experts see 34% more upside in healthcare stock after a technical breakout. Should you buy?
items.summary: Stock to buy for short-term: The healthcare stock has increased its bed capacity from 2,550 to 3,250, in the last three financial years.

items.title: India's power transmission sector to see INR 9 trillion capex push by 2032: Report
items.summary: India's T&D sector is poised for sustained growth, driven by an estimated INR 9 trillion capex through 2032. Despite temporary ordering slowdowns due to high capacity utilization and longer lead times for high-voltage transformers, a positive outlook remains. Global demand surges, particularly in the US and Europe, present significant export opportunities for Indian manufacturers.

items.title: BMW looking to double MINI sales in 2026, widening product portfolio, automaker's India chief says
items.summary: MINI plans to double its sales in India this year. This ambitious goal will be achieved through a wider range of vehicles, local production, and expanding its retail network. The company is introducing new models and special editions. MINI is also increasing its presence in new cities beyond the metros.

items.title: Star power meets style: Hrithik Roshan joins ONN as brand ambassador
items.summary: Lux Industries has appointed Hrithik Roshan as the brand ambassador for its premium innerwear and thermal wear brand, ONN. This strategic move aims to enhance brand recall and reach younger, aspirational consumers in urban and emerging markets. The collaboration underscores Lux Industries' commitment to building aspirational brands that blend comfort, performance, and style.

items.title: SW Solar shares rally 17% after Rs 3,500 crore order from Coal India. Check details
items.summary: Shares of Sterling and Wilson Renewable Energy Limited surged 17% after securing a Rs 3,490 crore solar EPC order from Coal India Limited, along with another project win. Strong order inflows and improved financial performance have boosted investor sentiment, driving sharp near-term gains in the stock.

items.title: Groww Q4 Results: Profit soars 122% YoY to Rs 686 crore, cashing in on market crash and Iran war
items.summary: Groww Q4 Results: Groww's parent company, Billionbrains Garage Ventures, announced a significant 122% year-on-year profit increase to Rs 686 crore for the March quarter. Revenue also surged 87% to Rs 1,505 crore. This strong performance was fueled by user growth and increased trading activity, especially in derivatives. Customer assets on the platform grew substantially, demonstrating continued investor confidence and platform engagement.

items.title: Markets near bottom, but volatility not over; buy in staggered manner: Sunil Subramaniam
items.summary: Markets are experiencing volatility due to global events. Experts advise investors to buy in small, staggered amounts. Institutional investors are gradually increasing positions. The upcoming earnings season will be crucial for determining the market's direction. Investors should focus on corporate guidance and sector-specific trends for future investment decisions.

items.title: UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank inevitable without change of course
items.summary: BRIEF-UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank 'inevitabl:BRIEF-UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank 'inevitable' without change of course

items.title: Jindal Stainless enters retail segment, expands into construction value chain
items.summary: Jindal Stainless Ltd has entered the retail market with Jindal Infinity, a new stainless steel rebar. This move allows the company to connect directly with builders and consumers. The product offers superior corrosion resistance for enhanced durability. Jindal Stainless is expanding its manufacturing capacity and network to meet growing demand for high-performance construction materials.

items.title: Lenskart shares tumble 5% amid online backlash over dress code row
items.summary: Lenskart shares fell amid social media backlash over an alleged dress code restricting religious symbols. The controversy persisted despite clarification from CEO Peyush Bansal, with the policy drawing criticism and boycott calls.

items.title: Why is stock market rising today? Sensex jumps 400 points, Nifty above 24,450. 4 key factors explained
items.summary: Indian markets reversed early losses to trade higher, supported by a stronger rupee, continued FII buying and oil prices staying below $100 per barrel. Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty gained around 0.5%, while broader markets also moved into positive territory led by PSU banks and midcap stocks.

items.title: JPMorgan finds Reliance Industries share valuation comfortable but flags O2C as uncertain spot
items.summary: JPMorgan maintains an Overweight rating on Reliance Industries. The brokerage sees comfortable relative valuation for RIL. Near-term outlook for oil-to-chemicals business remains uncertain. JPMorgan retains a price target of Rs 1,675 for March 2027. The firm expects improved refining and petrochemical margins in the medium term. Retail valuation upside also offers potential gains.

items.title: UP allows wheat sale at MSP without online registration
items.summary: Uttar Pradesh farmers can now sell wheat at government procurement centers without prior online registration. This decision comes after many farmers faced difficulties registering and were forced to sell to middlemen. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has ordered immediate implementation. Farmers can now sell their wheat as they did in previous years, ensuring fair prices for their produce.

items.title: Stock market today: Nifty 50 breaks above 24,400 resistance; next stop 25,000?
items.summary: Nifty 50 index hovers above the crucial resistance zone of 24,300 – 24,400, which remains a key hurdle for further upside. Analysts believe a sustained breakout above this range will be essential to strengthen bullish momentum and extend the rally towards the 24,800 – 25,000 levels.

items.title: Zydus Wellness extends RiteBite Max Protein into RTD shakes, ghee-jaggery bars and Korean chips
items.summary: On the NSE, Zydus Wellness shares traded at ₹500, up 0.15% on Monday, against a 52-week range of ₹331–₹552.40

items.title: Iran says it has not decided whether to attend peace talks with US
items.summary: Oil rises 5% as Tehran also threatens to retaliate for US seizure of cargo ship

items.title: Global Markets | Australian shares end largely unchanged as investors remain wary of Mideast risks
items.summary: Australian shares saw a modest rise on Monday. Investors remained cautious, awaiting developments in the Middle East conflict. Hopes for a quick resolution faded after Iran rejected talks and blocked the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. also seized an Iranian cargo ship. This uncertainty kept the market subdued, with gold miners and consumer firms providing some support.

items.title: IEX share price crashes 7.5% after this latest move by CERC. How to approach the stock now?
items.summary: IEX share price slumped to the day's low of ₹125.35 apiece in intraday deals today, as against its last closing price of ₹135.65 after CERC released new draft norms for market coupling.

items.title: Markets reverse early losses, Sensex climbs 377 points by midday; SBI, Trent lead gains
items.summary: The turnaround came despite a fragile global backdrop

items.title: Akshaya Tritiya witnessed buoyant sales across gems & jewellery despite high gold prices
items.summary: On April 19, the auspicious occasion drew large crowds at jewellery showrooms nationwide, with estimates indicating significant gold purchases despite a sharp price increase over the past year

items.title: India’s next midcap movers? 5 stocks flying below the radar
items.summary: As India’s economy accelerates, these five midcap companies are scaling quietly with strong growth, improving balance sheets and expansion plans worth tracking.

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Action Construction Equipment, Moil shares to buy
items.summary: Stock market opened slightly negative amid US-Iran negotiation uncertainties. By noon, Sensex rose 314.84 pts while Nifty 50 up by 98.85 points. Global oil prices surged, exacerbating inflation concerns, though smaller caps showed strong performance, with bullish recommendations for specific stocks.

items.title: India seeks to cool war’s oil burn with flex-fuel shift
items.summary: India is gearing up to fast-track the integration of flex-fuel vehicles into its automotive landscape. These innovative cars are capable of using higher blends of ethanol, paving the way for decreased dependence on imported oil. As the global oil markets grapple with instability stemming from geopolitical conflicts, this initiative is timely.

items.title: India expands Russian marine insurance options
items.summary: India has increased the number of Russian insurers authorized to provide marine cover for ships docking at its ports. This move allows more Russian companies to offer protection and indemnity coverage. The expanded list includes several Russian firms with registrations valid until 2026, 2027, 2029, and 2030.

items.title: Multibagger stock Suzlon Energy share price declines after a 5-day rally: Should you buy now?
items.summary: Suzlon Energy shares fell 2% on April 20 after a 20% surge in five sessions, as investors took profits. The stock hit a low of ₹51.85 despite a significant rise of over 15% in the past week and 1,100% over five years.

items.title: NDR InvIT to raise Rs 726.8 crore in preferential issue, accelerating drive to build India’s largest warehousing platform
items.summary: NDR InvIT is raising Rs 726.8 crore via preferential issue to fuel its expansion, following a recent Rs 410 crore bond issuance. This capital will fund acquisitions, including NDR Space Private Limited, and ongoing projects. The Trust has aggressively expanded its portfolio, adding significant warehousing space and entering new cities, solidifying its position as a leading logistics infrastructure platform.

items.title: Silver futures fall ₹5,175 to ₹2,51,967/kg
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, May silver contracts fell by ₹5,175 (2.01%) to ₹2,51,967 per kg, with a turnover of 1,505 lots

items.title: 4200% returns in five years! Multibagger stock hits upper circuit after listing on NSE
items.summary: Elitecon International's shares hit a 5% upper circuit at ₹45.68 on BSE after commencing trading on NSE on April 20, 2026, expanding its market presence. The stock has seen significant returns but faced recent declines, losing 71% in six months.

items.title: Lionsgate Play doubles down on theatrical-first strategy in India
items.summary: Lionsgate Play is shifting its India strategy to prioritize theatrical distribution, planning to release 10-12 premium Hollywood films annually starting September 2026 before their exclusive streaming debut. This theatrical-first approach aims to enhance visibility and audience engagement, offering a seamless journey from cinemas to its streaming platform.

items.title: Income tax rule changes from 1st April 2026. How will it impact the buyback of shares? Explained
items.summary: Buyback of shares: The income tax compliance has shifted from the dividend-based model to capital gains-based approach.

items.title: AI-first filmmaking model debuts with Jio Studios' 'Krishna'
items.summary: Jio Studios and Collective Artists Network have revealed the teaser for their new film Krishna. This movie uses an AI-native filmmaking process. The technology, called Galleri5, integrates artificial intelligence throughout production. This aims to improve efficiency and lower costs. It also makes advanced tools more accessible.

items.title: YES Bank, Vodafone Idea, Suzlon, Ola Electric among the most traded stocks today, 20 April 2026
items.summary: Most traded stocks today: YES Bank, Vodafone Idea, Suzlon Energy, Ola Electric Mobility, Jaiprakash Power Ventures (JP Power), and PC Jeweller were among the most traded stocks, or most active stocks in terms of volume, on the NSE.

items.title: Final batch of two 3,300 HP locomotives manufactured by BLW reach Mozambique
items.summary: Indian Railways has successfully completed its order of 10 locomotives to Mozambique, with the final two 3,300 HP diesel-electric locomotives manufactured at Banaras Locomotive Works reaching the nation. This 'Make in India, Make for the World' initiative showcases India's growing strength in global rail manufacturing, with these state-of-the-art locomotives designed for comfort and efficiency.

items.title: Global ad networks step up influencer marketing push in India
items.summary: Major advertising networks are boosting their presence in India's influencer marketing sector. They are investing in data-driven tools to meet advertiser demand for measurable results from creator campaigns. This shift aims to bring structure and clear performance insights to a rapidly growing market. Companies are consolidating to offer integrated creative, technology, and media services.

items.title: FII-DII data: FIIs turn net buyers for three straight sessions in Indian stock market; can the buying streak continue?
items.summary: FIIs recorded net buying for three consecutive days in April, amounting to ₹1,731.71 crore, following significant selling in previous months. Currency stabilisation and reduced speculation by the RBI may have influenced this change.

items.title: Office market stabilising; vacancies to decline marginally, rents to rise: Nuvama
items.summary: Office space vacancy in India is set to decrease slightly. Rental growth will improve as demand and supply trends balance. New office space additions are planned until 2028. Leasing activity has shown a strong recovery. Global Capability Centres are a key driver of demand.

items.summary: Tokenisation is taking hold in areas including money, bonds, gold, funds, real estate and private equity

items.summary: This will be Japan's first ever warship export project, and the first ship is scheduled to be delivered to the Royal Australian Navy in 2029.

items.title: South Korea's Lee to seek big boost in economic ties in summit with PM Modi
items.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is in New Delhi for talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The visit aims to significantly increase economic ties, focusing on shipbuilding. Both nations plan to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Cooperation may also expand into finance, artificial intelligence, and defense. India's role in global production is highlighted.

items.title: Inflation anxiety may have made a comeback, especially for low-income groups
items.summary: The latest survey by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) reveals growing inflation anxiety, with households’ perceptions and expectations of inflation rising. These perceptions are usually influenced by the food and fuel price outlook.

items.summary: Affluent Indians are shifting spending toward experiences, shrinking retail’s share even as premium purchases rise. Retailers are adding immersive formats to keep up.

items.title: Ads on tap: Why Jaquar still values old-school marketing in the age of AI
items.summary: While the bath fittings and lighting giant is leveraging AI for insights and footfall, it remains wary of ‘plastic’ generative visuals, choosing instead to double down on human-led branding and its legacy dealer network.

items.title: Tata Trusts affirms its faith in the CEO; it will review restrictive clauses that limit eligibility in the smaller trust
items.summary: After a meeting of the Bai Hirabai Jamsetji Tata Navsari Charitable board on Friday, Tata Trusts said its ethos is inclusive, secular, all-encompassing, and focused on philanthropy and national service.

items.summary: Cognizant's board of directors will monitor the use of AI tools and their financial impact on the company.

items.summary: The Iran-flagged Touska was on the U.S. Treasury's sanctions list because of a history of illegal activity, Trump said.

items.summary: Iran's top negotiator says his government has "no trust" in its enemies, after Trump says the U.S. won't be blackmailed.

items.summary: The airline said the "vast majority of customers" are being accommodated on same-day services.

items.title: Merz Sets Crisis Talks to Tackle Energy Price Impact on Germany
items.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he plans to convene Germany’s national security council to discuss the global energy crisis, signaling increased concern about its impact on Europe’s biggest economy.

items.title: Finance ministry may review MOOWR benefits for battery storage imports
items.summary: The Finance Ministry is set to discuss customs warehousing benefits for imported battery storage systems. Industry groups have raised concerns about the MOOWR scheme creating an uneven playing field. Consultations with power and renewable energy ministries are planned. The government is examining potential misuse and policy gaps. A decision will align with India's energy security goals.

items.title: Software stock dogs have joined market rally. There's a classic investing lesson in the rebound
items.summary: In a 2026 market that had Microsoft down close to 20%, looking for opportunities to buy after big drawdowns is coming to more tech and software stocks.

items.title: Gig workers social security scheme may have multiple fund managers
items.summary: The labour ministry of India is charting a new course by investigating a multi-manager framework for its latest social security initiative aimed at gig and platform workers. The EPFO is likely to manage the provident fund components, while various fund managers will look after pension allocations.

items.title: ‘Parsi-only’ rule in focus: Bai Hirabai Trust to move for change in ‘restrictive’ trustee clauses - Tata Trusts
items.summary: In a meeting on 17 April, the trustees reviewed the activities of the Bai Hirabai Trust and deliberated on the recent statements appearing in sections of the press relating to the trust

items.title: Nvidia's once-tight bond with gamers is cracking over AI
items.summary: Gamers once helped save Nvidia from bankruptcy. Now they feel left behind as the memory crunch drives focus to AI chips and DLSS 5 disrupts game design.

items.title: Modernizing the ledger: Inside the quest to solidify India’s economic data
items.summary: India’s GDP workings have been beset by critiques over the past decade. But new revisions aim to accurately capture the resilience of the country’s formalizing economy.

items.title: China’s Orient Securities Deal to Create $86 Billion Brokerage
items.summary: Two Shanghai government‑backed brokerages plan to merge in a deal that will create a firm with around $86 billion in assets, underscoring China’s push to consolidate the securities industry as it seeks to build world‑class investment banks.

items.title: US investors boost defence exposure as global wars fuel spending boom
items.summary: Trend reverses prior hesitance over arms investments because of slow growth and perceived conflict with ESG standards

items.title: Multiple states to borrow Rs 16,900 crore through RBI SGS auction on April 21
items.summary: Multiple state governments are set to raise a combined Rs 16,900 crore through a revised auction of State Government Securities on April 21. The Reserve Bank of India will conduct the auction on its E-Kuber platform, featuring fresh issuances and re-issues with varying tenors. Andhra Pradesh, Punjab, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, and Telangana are among the participating states.

items.title: CII suggests GIS-enabled land bank, uniform stamp duty to speed up acquisition
items.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has proposed a unified national industrial land bank with a GIS-enabled system to streamline land acquisition for manufacturing. The proposal includes standardized social impact assessments, district-level acquisition cells, and a dispute registry to reduce timelines and enhance transparency.

items.title: Govt may hike FDI limit in pension sector; Bill likely in Monsoon Session
items.summary: The government is poised to significantly boost foreign direct investment in the pension sector, potentially raising the limit to 100%. A bill to this effect is anticipated in upcoming parliamentary sessions. This move mirrors the insurance sector's recent FDI liberalization. Additionally, proposals include separating the NPS Trust from the PFRDA, aiming for independent management.

items.title: AI-proof your future with these 3 skills — Father of AGI says ‘human jobs will become obsolete’
items.summary: Ben Goertzel said a few roles, like educators and teachers, will remain intact – unaffected by the AGI because a human touch remains critical.

items.title: Tata Steel prepares for blue-collar job transitions as iron ore lease expiry nears
items.summary: With key iron ore blocks set for auction in 2030, the company is slowing hiring and warning workers of possible transitions.

items.title: India resilient amid oil shock, may grow 6.8%-7.1% despite global headwinds: SBI Research
items.summary: India faces global challenges from a strong position. Economic growth is projected between 6.8% and 7.1% for FY27. The nation's banking sector is robust. Opportunities arise for GIFT City and Indian airports. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain its current monetary policy stance. India's resilience is evident amidst regional conflict and global uncertainties.

items.title: IPO-bound Acko cuts 5% workforce as AI shift reshapes roles
items.summary: Acko has cut around 60 jobs amid an AI-led shift as CMO Ashish Mishra exits ahead of IPO plans

items.title: Wealth advisers made more than $2bn from private capital fees
items.summary: FT analysis of 16 funds shows extent of fees paid to banks and brokerages

items.title: BlackRock warns of hit to European stocks from energy crisis
items.summary: World’s biggest asset manager had been more bullish on region at start of year but says stocks are no longer cheap

items.title: France’s CB seeks to lead European fightback against Visa and Mastercard
items.summary: Payments scheme pushes for ‘co-badging’ that allows French bank cards to run on both international and local networks

items.title: Srinivas Narayanan, Kevin Weil, Bill Peebles: OpenAI lost 3 executives in one day as science division shuts down
items.summary: OpenAI lost Kevin Weil, Srinivas Narayanan and Bill Peebles in a single day as its science division was dissolved and Prism folded into Codex. Here's what happened.

items.title: Cabinet clears Rs 12,980 crore maritime insurance pool to cut costs
items.summary: India is launching the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool, backed by a substantial sovereign guarantee of over ₹12,980 crore. This strategic initiative aims to offer unwavering maritime insurance coverage for Indian vessels, encompassing all maritime risks and significantly decreasing dependency on international insurers.

items.title: 'Croatia, but cheaper': The quirky holiday spots on trend for 2026
items.summary: Montenegro and Albania are among the places rising in popularity for Britons seeking a holiday away from tourist traps.

items.title: Rupee depreciation forces Indian firms to adopt Yuan payments, boost local sourcing
items.summary: Indian businesses are actively seeking ways to counter the weakening rupee. Companies are now making payments for Chinese imports in yuan, aiming for better supplier prices. Simultaneously, there is a strong push towards sourcing more goods locally. This strategy helps reduce dependence on imported items and manage rising costs. These efforts are crucial for businesses facing currency pressures.

items.title: After bindi, tilak row, Lenskart releases dress code allowing religious symbols: 'Part of who we are'
items.summary: Eyewear retailer Lenskart has released a revised in-store style guide affirming that religious and cultural symbols are permitted, following allegations from employees and social media users that earlier guidelines led to penalties during audits.

items.title: HDFC Bank senior management backs Keki Mistry to continue as chairman
items.summary: For the quarter ended March, HDFC bank’s loan growth rose 12% on year to ₹3.17 trillion, higher than 5.4% on year growth witnessed in the previous year. Deposits continued to outpace credit at ₹3.91 trillion, up over 14% on year.

items.title: SMBC-backed Yes Bank poised for balance sheet expansion: MD Tonse
items.summary: Yes Bank posted a net profit of ₹1,068 crore for the March quarter, up 44.7% on year.
